Industrial Plant / Instrumentation

HPAL Nickel Plant Instrumentation Maintenance

Portfolio case study on field instrumentation work in HPAL nickel processing plant, covering calibration, loop checking, troubleshooting, shutdown support, and reliability maintenance.

Instrumentation / Control 2023-2024 PT Huayue Nickel Cobalt
instrument loop

Case study

OverviewChallengeRoleImpactDetails

DCS

loop check support

HPAL

nickel plant environment

Safety

excellence recognition

Problem / engineering challenge

HPAL plant operation depends on reliable instrumentation for pressure, flow, level, temperature, chemical measurement, and control valve performance. Field instruments must remain accurate and available in harsh industrial conditions.

My role

Performed installation, calibration, loop checking, preventive maintenance, corrective maintenance, troubleshooting, and shutdown support for instrumentation systems.

Outcome / impact

Supported plant reliability through disciplined field maintenance and safe instrumentation work practices.

Tools, systems, and references

Pressure transmitterFlow transmitterLevel transmitterRTDThermocouplepH/ORPControl valveDCS loop check

Context

The instrumentation role was carried out in an HPAL nickel cobalt processing environment, covering multiple plant areas such as autoclave, acid plant, water treatment, neutralization, MHP filter press, packaging, and tailing filter systems.

The work required strong field awareness, safety discipline, and hands-on technical capability.

Engineering Challenge

Instrumentation maintenance in a processing plant is highly practical: the engineer/technician must understand the signal, the process, the field device, the control system, and the safety implications of each activity.

  • Maintain measurement reliability for critical process variables.
  • Troubleshoot field device problems under plant operation constraints.
  • Perform calibration and loop check with correct documentation and communication.
  • Support shutdown work safely and efficiently.

Approach

The approach focused on practical field execution: identifying instrument problems, checking signal path, performing calibration, coordinating loop checks with DCS, and ensuring maintenance work was completed safely.

  • Worked with temperature, pressure, flow, level, pH, ORP, and control valve instruments.
  • Performed loop check and functional test activities with control room coordination.
  • Supported preventive and corrective maintenance to maintain instrument availability.

Impact

This project experience strengthens the portfolio because it shows direct field execution capability, not only office engineering. It demonstrates that engineering decisions are supported by practical understanding of instruments, plant operation, and maintenance constraints.
